summary: Convert large CSV data to chunked, compressed HDF5 format with proper metadata and efficient access patterns using h5py.
preconditions: HDF5 chunking enables partial I/O: only chunks overlapping the requested slice are read from disk
Compression is applied per-chunk: gzip gives best ratio, lzf gives best speed
strategy: Auto-detect column types from CSV, create separate HDF5 datasets for numeric (float64) and string (variable-length) columns
Set chunk size and compression (gzip/lzf) on each dataset for efficient partial reads and storage savings
Store column metadata as HDF5 attributes and create a _metadata group with file provenance information
AVOID: h5py variable-length strings require special dtype: h5py.special_dtype(vlen=str)
AVOID: Chunk size should match typical read patterns — too small causes overhead, too large wastes memory
